concept.headline: Object annotations enforce field presence and field types.
concept.explanation: TypeScript validates object shape during assignment and property access.
concept.syntax: const user: { name: string; age?: number } = { name: "Mira" };

demo.intro: Create a typed object with optional field.
demo.code:
const user: { name: string; age?: number } = { name: "Mira" };
console.log(user.name);

explanation.misconception: Optional field can be omitted safely

prediction.question: Is this valid? const p:{name:string}= {name:'x', age:2};
prediction.explanation: Object literal has extra property age not in target type.

explanation.afterDemo: Good object modeling catches API mismatches early.

practice.prompt: Define const item:{id:string; stock:number} and print id and stock.
practice.solutionCode:
const item: { id: string; stock: number } = { id: "p1", stock: 9 };
console.log(item.id, item.stock);

feedback.successMessage: Object shape and access are correct.

summary.title: Object Types — Complete
takeaways: Object types act as data contracts
